questions.question: Which property controls how content is distributed across columns in a multi-column layout?
questions.explanation: The column-fill property determines whether content is balanced across columns (auto lets columns fill sequentially, balance distributes content as evenly as possible).

questions.question: Which property prevents an element from being split across two columns?
questions.explanation: Setting break-inside: avoid on an element prevents the multi-column layout from breaking that element across column boundaries, keeping it whole in a single column.

questions.question: Which property forces a column break before an element?
questions.explanation: The break-before: column declaration forces a column break immediately before the element, pushing it to the start of the next column.

questions.question: What do the orphans and widows properties control in multi-column and paged layouts?
choices: The number of empty columns allowed
The minimum lines of text at the bottom and top of a column or page
The spacing between the first and last columns
The number of columns that must contain content
questions.explanation: The orphans property sets the minimum number of lines that must remain at the bottom of a column/page before a break. The widows property sets the minimum lines at the top of the next column/page.

questions.question: To force a column break after an element, use the property break-after with the value ___.
questions.explanation: Setting break-after: column forces the content following the element to move to the next column. Other values include avoid, page, and auto.

questions.question: The ___ property sets the minimum number of lines of a paragraph that must be visible at the bottom of a column before a break.
questions.explanation: The orphans property specifies how many lines of a block container must be left at the bottom of a column or page. This prevents single lines from being stranded before a break.
